page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv8-based AWS Graviton2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The server includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench software compilation with Clang scoring in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is highly competitive in the top 25% at 3794.5 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%, and the Passmark memory score is in the bottom 25%. Single-core performance is generally weaker, as evidenced by bottom-25% scores in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. It demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, making it well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving. However, it is less optimal for large language model inference, where text generation speeds for both small and large models fall into the bottom 10% to 25% of benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server equipped with 64 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering a high ratio of 16.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and small-block cached memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and large-block memory operations are more moderate. The server excels in Redis SET operations and static web serving, making it highly efficient for in-memory databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web applications. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton2 cores, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 Graviton2 cores, 1024 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server featuring one terabyte of memory and local NVMe storage for high-throughput in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton2 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and static web serving
Weaker single-core and large language model inference performance
